message:
perf(btree): add try_append_on_current_leaf() for zero-reload rightmost-leaf inserts

Add a specialized append path that writes a new cell directly onto the
leaf page already loaded at the top of the cursor stack, without going
through the full pager read + cell-pointer-array decode/reload cycle
that try_insert_on_leaf() requires.

try_append_on_current_leaf() reuses the cached page data and header
from the current stack entry:

1. Checks that the new cell fits: the gap between the end of the
   cell-pointer array (including the new 2-byte pointer) and the
   new content offset must be non-negative
2. Writes the cell data at the new content offset (growing downward)
3. Writes the 2-byte cell pointer at the end of the pointer array
4. Updates the header (cell_count, cell_content_offset) in-place
5. Stages the modified page through the pager in a single write_page_data
6. Extends the stack entry's cell_pointers Arc to include the new pointer

This is used in the rightmost-leaf hint path where the leaf was just
loaded and validated — the common case for monotonically-increasing
rowid INSERTs (autoincrement, sequential primary keys). The old path
called try_insert_on_leaf() which re-read the page from the pager and
re-parsed the cell pointer array, doubling the work on each insert.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
